title: Accreditation (Amendment) Act, 2011
text:
The Accreditation (Amendment) Act, 2011 4 No. 2 of 2011 ANTIGUA AND BARBUDA
[ L.S.]
I Assent,
Louise Lake-Tack,
Governor-General.
1st June, 2011.
ANTIGUA AND BARBUDA
THE ACCREDITATION (AMENDMENT) ACT, 2011
THE ACCREDITATION (AMENDMENT) ACT, 2011
No. 2 of 2011
No. 2 of 2011
AN ACT to amend the Accreditation Act 2006, Act No. 4 of 2006. [ Printed in the Official Gazette Vol. XXXI No.32
dated 16th June, 2011. ]
ENACTED by the Parliament of Antigua and Barbuda as follows:
1. Short title
This Act may be cited as the Accreditation (Amendment) Act, 2011.
2. Interpretation
In this Act “the principal Act” means the Accreditation Act 2006, Act No. 4 of 2006.
3. Insertion of the definition of the word “principal”
The principal Act is amended in section 2 by inserting the following definition after the word
“Minister”—
“principal” means the person or body of persons responsible for the general administration of
an institution offering tertiary education in Antigua and Barbuda;”. ________
Printed at the Government Printing Office, Antigua and Barbuda,
4. Amendment of section 14-Functions of the Board by Paget Terry, Acting Government Printer
— By Authority, 2011.
The principal Act is amended in section 14 (1) (c) by inserting the words “of higher education” after
the word “institutions”. 800—06.11 [ Price $2.60 ]

D. Gisele Isaac-Arrindell, Hazlyn M. Francis,
Speaker. President. “40 Contravention by institution
(1) Where an institution is in contravention of section 39 the Board shall in writing serve notice on
Romona Small, Romona Small,
the principal of the institution containing the nature of the contravention and the period of time within
Clerk to the House of Representatives.(Ag.) Clerk to the Senate.(Ag.)
which the contravention is required to be rectified.
(2) Where an institution is in contravention of subsection (2) and the contravention is not rectified
in the time stipulated within the notice served, the Board may recommend to the Minister that the
name of the institution be removed from the Register.”.
